description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a rectangular body featuring a brushed metal top and front panel, complemented by wooden side panels. The front face is equipped with multiple rotary knobs, toggle switches, and a large tuning dial, as well as a horizontal frequency display illuminated in blue, and a small analog meter. The top surface has two sets of ventilation slits for heat dissipation. The rear panel includes a variety of RCA input/output ports and speaker terminals, clearly labeled for left and right channels. The overall design is robust and functional, with a retro aesthetic that suggests it is intended for home or studio audio use. The materials and build indicate durability and a focus on both utility and style.
description: A vintage radio receiver with a brushed metal front, wooden sides, multiple control knobs, and a blue-lit frequency display. The back features numerous audio ports and speaker terminals.
description_view_0: Front view showing the control panel with knobs, switches, a large tuning dial, and a blue-lit frequency display.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the wooden panel and the protruding control knobs on the front.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the RCA input/output ports and speaker terminals for left and right channels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a plain wooden panel and visible control knob protrusions.
